methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Defense & Security (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 20 same-sector peers exist in Defense & Security, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Red 6, a Series B+ stage defense and security company that has raised $100M+, operates within a fragmented frontier-tech sector where precise peer benchmarking is constrained by data scarcity. The broader Defense & Security universe comprises 20 known private companies and 2 public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS), but the cohort of same-stage or adjacent-stage peers meeting valuation-tier criteria falls below the minimum threshold of three datapoints required for reliable peer-median analysis.

The peer-median approach is not applicable here due to insufficient narrow peer clustering. While 8 private-stage companies and 2 public companies exist within the sector (including Anduril Industries, Saronic, Flock Safety, Shield AI, and Chaos Industries), none satisfy the conjunction of stage, valuation proximity, and business model alignment needed to generate a defensible median multiple. Attempting an all-sector median would introduce excessive dispersion and mask critical differences in technology maturity, customer concentration, and defense contract dependencies that vary materially across the comp set.

Without triangulation across multiple methods, a precise valuation range cannot be supported by this dataset. The analysis highlights two key uncertainties: (1) the absence of a tightly clustered peer group limits benchmarking confidence, and (2) public comparables carry distinct risk profiles (regulatory, geopolitical, contract concentration) that may not translate directly to a private, earlier-stage operator. Further data on revenue multiples, customer concentration, and milestone achievement across the identified peer set would be required to establish a credible valuation envelope.
